calendar.matters:
Motion For Peremptory Writ, Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 1094.5
Off Calendar Oct-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael J. Deniro of the law offices of Michael J. Deniro (mjdeniro@cox.net) appears as counsel for moving party, Petitioner

Attorney, Jean-Paul P. Cart of the law offices of Venable LLP (JPCart@Venable.com) appears as counsel for opposing party, Respondent The Regents of the University of California

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

PETITIONER JANE ROE Motion For Peremptory Writ, Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 1094.5 is DENIED per written order signed and filed Oct-30-2020. See written order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.

Motion For Peremptory Writ, Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 1094.5
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Continued To Oct-30-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Motion To Disqualify Judge Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 170.6
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER JANE ROE's Motion To Disqualify Judge Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 170.6:

The peremptory challenge is granted. No hearing will be held. The October 29, 2020 hearing on petitioner's petition for writ of administrative mandate is continued from October 29 to October 30, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. to be heard by Judge Richard B. Ulmer.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion To Disqualify Judge Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 170.6
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

PETITIONER JANE ROE's Motion To Disqualify Judge Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 170.6 is Off calendar. The Court has issued an order striking the statement of disqualification. There is no need for or entitlement to a hearing on the matter.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
